howItWorks.intro: This music royalty calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Revenue = Streams × Pay Rate × Your Split %
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much does Spotify pay per stream?
faq.answer: Spotify pays approximately $0.003-$0.005 per stream, varying by country and listener subscription type. The average is about $0.004. Apple Music pays roughly double at $0.008.

faq.question: Is the Music Royalty Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard music and audio formulas trusted by musicians and audio engineers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What monthly streams should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate monthly streams value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Music Royalty Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the Music Royalty Calculator?
sections.body: The Music Royalty Calculator is a free online tool designed for musicians and audio engineers who need quick, accurate calculations in the music and audio space. By entering your monthly streams, platform, your split, you get instant results including monthly gross, monthly net, annual net. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting monthly gross right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In music and audio, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by musicians and audio engineers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to fine-tune your sound with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your monthly streams and need to find the right monthly gross. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Monthly Streams
howToSteps.description: Type or select your monthly streams in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Platform
howToSteps.description: Type or select your platform in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Your Split (%)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your your split in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your monthly gross, monthly net, annual net. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your monthly streams before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both monthly gross and monthly net — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to fine-tune your sound.
If you're unsure about your your split, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
